# Scheduled Maintenance

> Define recurring maintenance schedules for vehicles based on calendar intervals or odometer milestones, track due dates, trigger work orders, and record completion to advance the schedule.

**Category:** Workflow · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** fleet · vehicle · maintenance · scheduling · reminders · odometer · preventive

## What this does

Define recurring maintenance schedules for vehicles based on calendar intervals or odometer milestones, track due dates, trigger work orders, and record completion to advance the schedule.

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **vehicle** *(text, required)* — Vehicle
- **task_name** *(text, required)* — Task Name
- **maintenance_type** *(select, required)* — Maintenance Type
- **trigger_type** *(select, required)* — Trigger Type
- **interval_days** *(number, optional)* — Calendar Interval (days)
- **interval_km** *(number, optional)* — Odometer Interval (km)
- **start_date** *(date, required)* — Schedule Start Date
- **end_date** *(date, optional)* — Schedule End Date
- **last_completed_date** *(date, optional)* — Last Completed Date
- **last_completed_odometer** *(number, optional)* — Last Completed Odometer (km)
- **next_due_date** *(date, optional)* — Next Due Date
- **next_due_odometer** *(number, optional)* — Next Due Odometer (km)
- **assigned_to** *(text, optional)* — Assigned To
- **advance_warning_days** *(number, optional)* — Advance Warning (days)
- **status** *(select, required)* — Status

## What must be true

- **interval_required:** At least one of interval_days or interval_km must be provided — a schedule with neither is invalid
- **date_based_next_due:** When trigger_type is date-based, next_due_date = last_completed_date (or start_date) + interval_days
- **odometer_based_next_due:** When trigger_type is odometer-based, next_due_odometer = last_completed_odometer + interval_km
- **both_trigger_earliest:** When trigger_type is both, the task becomes due when either threshold is first reached
- **auto_cancel_past_end_date:** If end_date is set and next_due_date exceeds end_date, the schedule is automatically cancelled
- **daily_job:** System runs a daily job to transition active → due_soon → overdue based on next_due_date
- **odometer_reevaluation:** Odometer-based due dates are re-evaluated whenever a new odometer reading is recorded for the vehicle
- **completion_requires_reference:** Task completion is only valid with a service log reference or completion date

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Odometer Due Triggered** — when trigger_type includes odometer; vehicle current odometer is >= next_due_odometer; status is active, then Task transitions to due_soon because the odometer threshold has been reached.
- **Task Completed** — when status is due_soon, overdue, or active; completion date and service log reference are provided, then Completion is recorded, schedule advances to the next cycle and becomes active.
- **Task Overdue** — when status is due_soon or active; next_due_date has passed, then Task is marked overdue and an alert is sent to the fleet manager.
- **Task Due Soon** — when system daily scan runs; status is active; days until next_due_date is less than or equal to advance_warning_days, then Task transitions to due_soon and the assignee is notified.
- **Schedule Created** — when vehicle exists in the fleet; task_name and maintenance_type are provided; at least one of interval_days or interval_km is provided; start_date is valid, then Maintenance schedule is active with next due date and/or odometer computed.

## Errors it can return

- `SCHEDULE_NO_INTERVAL` — A maintenance schedule must have either a calendar interval or an odometer interval.
- `SCHEDULE_INVALID_DATE` — Schedule start date is invalid.

## Connects to

- **vehicle-master-data** *(required)* — Vehicle master provides last odometer reading for odometer-based interval calculations
- **vehicle-maintenance-log** *(required)* — Completing a scheduled task creates a maintenance log record and advances the schedule
- **odometer-tracking** *(recommended)* — Validated odometer readings trigger odometer-based due date re-evaluation
